name: model-adapter
summary: Two-way translator between a model and its array representation, for storage and transport.
description: Creates a model adapter for converting between models and arrays
problem: Your model is a typed in-memory shape, but storage and transport (JSON, database rows, queue messages) speak associative arrays. You need a single, well-named place to hold that translation in both directions.
appliesWhen: A model needs to flow into storage as rows or out as JSON/array data
You want one canonical translation point instead of scattered conversion logic
Both directions of the conversion are needed (read and write)
The shape is stable enough to encode once
avoidWhen: Conversion only happens in one direction (use a one-way mapper or builder)
The model's array form is identical to its properties — no translation needed
You're converting between two different domain types — that's not an adapter, that's a transform
examples: Add an adapter for the Order model
I need to serialize a Subscription to and from arrays
Create a translator between Payout objects and DB rows
tradeoffs: One file dedicated to bidirectional conversion. Adds a step between model and storage but keeps that logic in one place. Convention is one adapter per model — uniformity over per-context customization.
outputs: A {name} adapter class with toArray() and fromArray() conversions
References to the model FQCN for type safety
postApply: Implement toArray() for the read path (model -> array)
Implement fromArray() for the write path (array -> model)
Make sure both directions cover the same fields

model.aiHint: Use the FQCN of the model class (created by phpnomad/model). Convention is {namespace}\Models\{name}.
